hints.text: Plate white + blue strip left + **yellow strip right** (unique; discontinued 2020, so Gen4 may show only the blue)
**Portuguese**: Ç Ã Õ Â Ê and **no Ñ** (Spain has Ñ); road = **Rua** (Galician = Rúa)
**Eucalyptus** trees very common (only other part of Europe with them = northern Spain)
Concrete **ladder poles with very tall steps** (each with a small see-through hole) — Spain/France use short steps
Hunting signs **red-and-white** (Spain = black/white); chevrons **black w/ yellow arrows** (only Mediterranean country with these)
Bollards wedge-shaped w/ thin white top, or flat w/ a wide reflector (orange reflector = darker than Spain's yellow)
Directional signs white w/ **black arrows not touching the border**; km markers = wide rectangle w/ a diagonal coloured section
Pedestrian sign **5 stripes, person with a high belt**; stop sign large font (Spain compact); road signs have a **white border**
**Cobblestone** roads (square stones, diagonal) + bright cobblestone sidewalks; white stone houses w/ orange roofs & blue/yellow window trim
Landline area codes = first 3 digits (map); codes not starting with 2 aren't regional; **29 = Azores or Madeira**
**Faro (south)** decorative round/square chimneys; oak fields SE; olives east; **Douro** = steep terraced vineyards east of Porto
**Madeira & Azores** count as Portugal; Serra da Estrela = rocky highland w/ yellow-black striped snow poles; Porto = lime-green street signs
